name: Blueprints
description: Save and reuse common API request structures as blueprints. Insert them anywhere with a slash command.
author: Parv Bajaj
features: Create blueprints from scratch or copy from any existing .void file
Blueprints are native .void files — no custom format or lock-in
Shared blueprints live in .blueprints/ and are committed to Git
Local blueprints live in .voiden/blueprints/ and stay off Git
Toggle shared/local per blueprint with a single click
Conflict detection — blueprints that clash with the current section are disabled in the slash menu
Always reads fresh content from disk on every invocation
Enable or disable individual blueprints without deleting them
Search and filter blueprints in the manager

buttons.description: Opens the Blueprints Manager as a tab in the main editor

ui.description: Registers a status bar button that opens the Blueprints Manager

slashCommands.description: Registers a /bp-<name> slash command for each blueprint. Commands are auto-generated from filenames and respect singleton block constraints per section.

readme:
# Blueprints

Blueprints lets you save reusable API request structures as `.void` files and insert them into any page with a slash command.

## How it works

1. Click **Blueprints** in the status bar to open the manager
2. Create a new blueprint from scratch, or copy content from an existing `.void` file
3. Give it a name — the file is saved as `blueprint-<name>.void` and a `/bp-<name>` slash command is created automatically
4. Open any page, type `/bp-` and select your blueprint to insert it

## Shared vs Local

| | Path | Git |
|---|---|---|
| **Shared** | `.blueprints/` | Committed — shared with your team |
| **Local** | `.voiden/blueprints/` | Ignored — stays on your machine |

Toggle between shared and local at any time from the manager or directly on each card.

## Conflict detection

Blueprints that contain a block type that already exists in the current section (e.g. an endpoint block when one is already present) are automatically disabled in the slash menu. This prevents duplicate singleton blocks.

## File naming

- File on disk: `blueprint-get-user.void`
- Slash command: `/bp-get-user`
- Display name: **Blueprint Get User**

## Tips

- After editing a blueprint file directly in the editor, the next slash command invocation always reads the latest saved version — no refresh needed.
- Use the **↻** button in the manager if newly created files aren't appearing in the list.
- Blueprints can be enabled or disabled individually without deleting them.
